pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4553), ChemicalBook, NIST, EPA databases
CAMEO Chemicals: 'If the polymerization takes place inside a closed container, the container may rupture violently.' Storage hazard above 32°C, self-sustaining above 95°C.
CAMEO Chemicals and multiple SDS sources. Heat (>95°C), peroxides (di-tert-butyl, dibenzoyl), catalysts (butyllithium, azo compounds). Inhibitor is NOT a trigger—inhibitors PREVENT polymerization.
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/28), ChemicalBook, EPA, BASF SDS
CAMEO Chemicals: 'The uninhibited acid polymerizes exothermically at ambient temperature and explodes if confined.'
CAMEO Chemicals documents heat, peroxides, UV radiation, and chemical reactants (bases, amines, ammonia). Inhibitor depletion can trigger polymerization but inhibitor itself does not trigger it.
CAMEO Chemicals: 'Hazardous polymerization can be initiated by organic and inorganic peroxides; azo compounds; redox systems; light; and high energy radiation.'
CAMEO Chemicals confirms heat, light, peroxides. Additional triggers: organic/inorganic peroxides, azo compounds, high energy radiation.
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8), EPA, CDC, NIOSH, multiple SDS

CAMEO Chemicals states: Not flagged with (P) designation for explosive polymerization. Multiple SDS confirm 'Hazardous polymerization will not occur.' Polymerization only occurs at extreme lab conditions (-96°C with catalyst).
CAMEO Chemicals and multiple SDS confirm no polymerization hazard under normal storage and handling.
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/7108), EPA, CDC, NIOSH, BASF SDS
CAMEO Chemicals: 'May polymerize if contaminated or subjected to heat... If the polymerization takes place inside a container, the container may rupture violently.'
CAMEO Chemicals confirms heat, light, peroxides. Additional triggers include contamination and strong acids/bases. Inhibitor depletion can trigger but inhibitor itself does not.
EPA Toluene toxicological review: 'Hazardous polymerization has not been reported.' CAMEO Chemicals does not mention polymerization hazard. Multiple SDS confirm no polymerization concern.
CAMEO Chemicals and multiple SDS confirm no polymerization hazard.

pi_items.verified_value: CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/) - a legitimate NOAA resource for chemical hazard data
pi_items.source: All chemical datasheets traced to CAMEO Chemicals website URLs directly fetched and verified

privileged_info_summary: All six chemicals have correct CAS numbers and correct polymerization status (YES for styrene, acrylic acid, vinyl acetate, methyl methacrylate; NO for acetone and toluene). The primary triggers are accurately identified for most chemicals: heat, light, and peroxides are confirmed polymerization triggers across susceptible monomers. However, the metadata incorrectly lists 'inhibitor' as a trigger for styrene, acrylic acid, and methyl methacrylate—inhibitors actually PREVENT polymerization, not trigger it (though inhibitor depletion can allow polymerization to resume). The data is real and sourced from NOAA's CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), a public authoritative database for chemical hazard information, not synthetic data.
